task_completion.description: Navigate to the Wikipedia article for 'Turing machine'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open with the address bar showing 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turing_machine' and the Wikipedia page for Turing machine is loaded.
The browser's print dialog or print preview screen is visible over the Wikipedia page.

task_completion.description: Configure print settings to remove headers and footers
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the print dialog settings (often under 'More settings'), the checkbox for 'Headers and footers' is unchecked or disabled.

task_completion.description: Configure print settings to remove background graphics
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the print dialog settings, the checkbox for 'Background graphics' is unchecked or disabled.

task_completion.description: Save the output PDF to the specified Desktop path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file manager shows the file being saved as 'turing_print_audit.pdf' in the '/home/ga/Desktop/' directory, or the file is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Verify the PDF preserves the main article text
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser's print preview or the opened PDF shows the main article text, including phrases like 'Turing machine' and 'mathematical model of computation'.

task_completion.description: Verify the print stylesheet correctly hides sidebar navigation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser's print preview or the opened PDF does NOT show standard Wikipedia sidebar elements like 'Donate to Wikipedia', 'Current events', 'Random article', or 'Upload file'.

integrity.description: The agent used a real web browser's print functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a graphical web browser's print dialog, rather than using terminal commands (like wkhtmltopdf or pandoc) to bypass the browser's print stylesheet rendering.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create a fake PDF document
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not open a word processor (like LibreOffice Writer) to paste text and export as PDF.

integrity.description: The agent relied on the print stylesheet, not manual DOM manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use browser Developer Tools to manually delete the sidebar DOM elements before printing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turing_machine. Output Path: /home/ga/Desktop/turing_print_audit.pdf. Required PDF content (main text): 'Turing machine', 'mathematical model of computation'. Forbidden PDF content (sidebar navigation that must be hidden by print stylesheet): 'Donate to Wikipedia', 'Current events', 'Random article', 'Upload file'. Print settings must have 'Headers and footers' and 'Background graphics' disabled.
